item.title: Indonesia Vows ‘Smart Interventions’ as Rupiah Falls
item.description: Indonesia’s central bank pledged “smart interventions” in the foreign-exchange markets as the rupiah fell to a record low.

item.title: Trump Tower Plan for Australia’s Gold Coast Scrapped
item.description: The Trump Organization has scrapped plans to put its name to a tower on Australia’s Gold Coast, about three months after announcing a deal to bring the brand’s first hotel to the country.

item.title: Philippine Bonds Face Extended Slump on Large Rate Hike Bets
item.description: Philippine sovereign bonds are extending a selloff as traders price in the prospect of a 50-basis-point rate hike, the largest since 2023.

item.title: Geothermal Energy Developer Fervo Raises $1.89 Billion in US IPO
item.description: Fervo Energy Co., a geothermal energy developer, raised $1.89 billion in a US initial public offering that priced above the marketed range, after upsizing the deal earlier this week.

item.title: Nidec Drops 18% After Flagging Quality Conduct Concerns
item.description: Nidec Corp. shares slumped as much as 18% in Tokyo on Wednesday after the company said it identified suspected improper conduct on product quality.

item.title: Alibaba, Tencent AI Bets Face ‘Show Me the Profits’ Scrutiny
item.description: Chinese investors are heading into the earnings season demanding proof that billions in AI spending by tech giants is paying off.

item.title: Hedge Fund CFM Opens Office in Shanghai, Assets Top $27 Billion
item.description: French quantitative hedge fund Capital Fund Management has opened an office in Shanghai, following in the footsteps of peers in establishing a presence in the world’s second-largest economy.

item.title: China’s AI Suppliers Can’t Keep Up as Component Shortages Bite
item.description: China’s artificial intelligence hardware suppliers have a problem: keeping up with insatiable demand for their products.

item.title: AI Life Science Firm Metis TechBio Set for HK Debut After IPO
item.description: Metis TechBio Co, a biotechnology company using artificial intelligence to deliver and formulate drugs, is set to begin trading in Hong Kong after raising HK$2.1 billion ($270 million) in an initial public offering.

item.title: Gold Holds Decline as US Inflation Jump Lowers Rate Cut Odds
item.description: Gold was steady after US data showed an acceleration in inflation that lowers the odds of the Federal Reserve reducing interest rates this year.

item.title: Goldman Sees Dollar Strength as Energy Shock to Keep Rates High
item.description: The dollar strength will build further over the near term as the energy-price shock will keep yields elevated as economic growth remains relatively resilient, according to Goldman Sachs Group.

item.title: AI Chipmaker Cerebras to Guide IPO Pricing Above Range
item.description: Cerebras Systems Inc. is guiding prospective investors that it expects to price its initial public offering above the top of its marketed range, according to people familiar with the matter, as demand soars for the chipmaker’s stock.

item.title: Asian Stocks Under Pressure as US Inflation Rises: Markets Wrap
item.description: Asian stocks are poised for a weak open after losses on Wall Street, with the dollar strengthening and bonds falling as inflation data showed the impact of energy disruptions linked to the war in Iran.

item.title: Oil Holds Gain With Iran’s Exports Strained as Conflict Drags On
item.description: Oil was steady as the conflict in the Middle East drags on and keeps the vital Strait of Hormuz effectively closed, with shipments from Iran’s main export terminal appearing to come to a standstill.

item.title: Hedge Fund Dymon’s Assets Near $8 Billion, Blowing Past Estimate
item.description: Hedge fund firm Dymon Asia Capital Pte is on track to reach $8 billion in assets under management by the third quarter as more global investors seek to back Asia-based hedge funds, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Mexico Rating Outlook Revised to Negative at S&P as Debts Mount
item.description: S&P Global Ratings revised Mexico’s credit outlook to negative from stable, citing persistently weak fiscal results, rising debt levels and weak economic growth.

item.title: Ananym Capital Urges BWX to Make Commercial Nuclear Reactors
item.description: Activist fund Ananym Capital Management is pushing nuclear technology company BWX Technologies Inc. to tap its nuclear reactor manufacturing capabilities for commercial use.

item.title: Nvidia CEO Pay Package Shrinks 27% on Smaller Stock Awards
item.description: The total pay package for Nvidia Corp. Chief Executive Officer Jensen Huang decreased 27% to $36.3 million in fiscal 2026 after the value of his stock awards declined.

item.title: Anthropic In Talks to Raise $30 Billion at $900 Billion Valuation
item.description: Anthropic PBC is in early talks with investors to raise at least $30 billion in fresh financing, according to people familiar with the matter, setting the stage for what could be its largest funding round yet.

item.title: Japan’s Sojitz Eyes Southeast Asia for New Rare Earths Supply
item.description: Japanese trading house Sojitz Corp. is looking to Southeast Asia and other regions as new potential sources of rare earths outside Australia, as it aims to boost output and diversify its supply chain of the highly sought-after materials.

item.title: China’s Manufacturing Heartland Tested by Iran War Energy Shock
item.description: Economic activity in China’s most important manufacturing hub is heaping pressure on power supplies, sparking an energy stress test as the Iran war chokes shipments of fuel.

item.title: CommScope Sued by Lenders for at Least $150 Million Over Alleged Breach
item.description: CommScope Holding Co. was sued by a group of lenders who allege the telecommunications provider breached a debt agreement by failing to pay a prescribed premium of at least $150 million following a key asset sale.

item.title: Bond Bears Reload Fed Rate Hike Wagers on Stubborn Inflation
item.description: Traders have renewed bearish bets on US Treasuries, lifting expectations that the Federal Reserve will raise interest rates as oil prices and inflation push higher.

item.title: American Electric Power Utility Offers $2.6 Billion in Stock
item.description: American Electric Power Co., one of the biggest utilities in the US, is seeking to raise $2.6 billion in a share sale as artificial intelligence technology drives booming demand for electricity.

item.title: Chip Stocks Drag Market Lower in Worrying Sign for Momentum Bet
item.description: High-flying chip stocks struggled through one of their most challenging sessions of the year on Tuesday in a dramatic reversal for some of the market’s best performers.

item.title: Family Offices Build Stake in Niche Miner After Stock Soars 900%
item.description: For decades, the risky world of early-stage mining ventures was the playground of institutional funds and commodity trading houses. But after a more than 900% rally in a little-known Canadian potash developer last year, a new class of investors is moving in: family offices.

item.title: Contrarian 5% Bet on 10-Year Treasuries Is Gaining Credibility
item.description: As US Treasury traders debate whether the 30-year yield will mount a sustained push past 5%, one market veteran is pushing a far bolder wager.

item.title: Small Businesses Say Hiring Is Still Brutal
item.description: No letup on inflation or labor.

item.title: Apple Plans Customizable Camera for Pros, Siri Design Changes in iOS 27
item.description: Apple Inc. is planning to upgrade its Camera app, making the software fully customizable as part of a broader set of user interface changes coming in its next iPhone software update.

item.title: How India’s Modi Has Become More Powerful Than Ever
item.description: A stunning election victory for Narendra Modi’s party shows how he’s consolidating power, even with the economy under pressure.
